creator: mux@qualys.com
text:
HTDump is a tool used to retrieve www data.

PS: I already sent this port to ports@FreeBSD.org, please ignore this
mail since I was using MAKE_ARGS instead of ALL_TARGET.

Fix: 

Xpre-extract:
X.if !defined(WITH_SSL)
X       @${ECHO} "Define WITH_SSL to build with SSL support"--flVAhuCHT9r4WomQUADYkwlR7UFdxPWC4pObaV7OcCB3rQss
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.shar"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.shar"

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#       htdump
#       htdump/Makefile
#       htdump/distinfo
#       htdump/pkg-descr
#       htdump/pkg-plist
#       htdump/pkg-comment
#       htdump/files
#       htdump/files/patch-aa
#
echo c - htdump
mkdir -p htdump > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - htdump/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/Makefile << 'END-of-htdump/Makefile'
X# Ports collection makefile for:  htdump
X# Date created:                   Tue Feb 20, 2001
X# Whom:                           Maxime Henrion (mux@qualys.com)X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=      htdump
XPORTVERSION=   0.9x
XCATEGORIES=    www
XMASTER_SITES=  http://arak.cs.hro.nl/~ren/linux/htdump/bin/
X
XMAINTAINER=    mux@qualys.com
X
XLIB_DEPENDS=    gnugetopt.1:${PORTSDIR}/devel/libgnugetopt
X
XUSE_GMAKE=     yes
X
X.if defined(WITH_SSL)
XUSE_OPENSSL=   yes
XALL_TARGET=    ssl
X.endif
XX.endif
X
Xdo-install:
X       ${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/htdump ${PREFIX}/bin
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-htdump/Makefile
echo x - htdump/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/distinfo << 'END-of-htdump/distinfo'
XMD5 (htdump-0.9x.tar.gz) = a7a213b81af05399cc57f9bad5a082ea
END-of-htdump/distinfo
echo x - htdump/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/pkg-descr << 'END-of-htdump/pkg-descr'
XHTdump is an automation utility to retrieve URLs and write them to a file, or
Xprocess them through a pipeline. It is also a very usefull tool for web
Xdeveloper who work low level, for example with CGI binaries. This makes a
Xperfect debugging tool, since it allows usual but also rfc-illegal operations.
XPacket level debugging is included in this tool.
X
XWWW:   http://arak.cs.hro.nl/~ren/linux/
END-of-htdump/pkg-descr
echo x - htdump/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/pkg-plist << 'END-of-htdump/pkg-plist'X@comment $FreeBSD$
Xbin/htdump
END-of-htdump/pkg-plist
echo x - htdump/pkg-comment
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/pkg-comment << 'END-of-htdump/pkg-comment'
XHTdump is a tool to retrieve WWW data
END-of-htdump/pkg-comment
echo c - htdump/files
mkdir -p htdump/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - htdump/files/patch-aa
sed 's/^X//' >htdump/files/patch-aa << 'END-of-htdump/files/patch-aa'
X*** Makefile.orig      Tue Dec 19 16:26:47 2000
X--- Makefile   Tue Feb 20 12:28:55 2001
X***************
X*** 5,11 ****
X  #
X  #
X  
X! CFLAGS       = -O4 -m486 -Wall #-funsigned-char -Wshadow -Wmissing-prototypes -Wmissing-declarations -Winline
X  PROGS        = htdump
X  LFLAGS       = -s #-static
X  CC   = cc
X--- 5,11 ----
X  #
X  #
X  
X! CFLAGS       = -O4 -Wall -I${PREFIX}/include #-funsigned-char -Wshadow -Wmissing-prototypes -Wmissing-declarations -Winline
X  PROGS        = htdump
X  LFLAGS       = -s #-static
X  CC   = cc
X***************
X*** 22,32 ****
X  
X  htdump:              $(OBJS) socket.o
X               rm -f htdump
X!              $(CC) $(LFLAGS) -o htdump $(OBJS) socket.o
X  
X  ssl:         $(OBJS) socket-ssl.o
X               rm -f htdump
X!              $(CC) $(LFLAGS) -o htdump $(OBJS) socket-ssl.o -lssl -lcrypto -L/usr/local/ssl/lib
X  
X  $(OBJS):     %.o: %.c $(HDRS)
X  X--- 22,32 ----
X  
X  htdump:              $(OBJS) socket.o
X               rm -f htdump
X!              $(CC) $(LFLAGS) -o htdump $(OBJS) socket.o -lgnugetopt -L${PREFIX}/lib
X  
X  ssl:         $(OBJS) socket-ssl.o
X               rm -f htdump
X!              $(CC) $(LFLAGS) -o htdump $(OBJS) socket-ssl.o -lssl -lcrypto -lgnugetopt -L/usr/local/ssl/lib -L${PREFIX}/lib
X  
X  $(OBJS):     %.o: %.c $(HDRS)
X  
END-of-htdump/files/patch-aa
exit
